atten4.ptxt: By reading the DATA LIST displayed on an intelligent tester, values can be checked, including those of the switches, sensors, and actuators, without removing any parts. Reading the DATA LIST as the first step in troubleshooting is one method to shorten diagnostic time.

atten3.ptxt: In the table below, the values listed under Normal Condition are for reference only. Do not depend solely on these values when determining whether or not a part is faulty.

step2.ptxt: Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Turn the ignition switch to the ON position.
Select the following menu items: DIAGNOSIS / ENHANCED OBD II / DATA LIST.
Check the values by referring to the table below.

atten4.ptxt: If no idling conditions are specified, the transmission gear selector lever should be in the N or P position, and the A/C switch and all accessory switches should be off.

entry.ptxt: Ignition timing advance for No. 1 cylinder:

item.ptxt: 10 to 30%: Idling
10 to 30%: Running without load at 2,500 rpm

entry.ptxt: Load percentage in terms of maximum intake air flow amount
Air flow rate from Mass Air Flow (MAF) meter:
2 to 6 g/sec.: Running without load at 2,500 rpm

item.ptxt: MAF meter power source circuit open
VG circuit open or shorted

entry.ptxt: If value is 160.0 g/sec. or more:

item.ptxt: E2G circuit open
If -40°C (-40°F): sensor circuit open
If 140°C (284°F) or more: sensor circuit shorted
If -40°C (-40°F): sensor circuit open
If 140°C (284°F) or more: sensor circuit shorted
Less than 1 (0 to 0.999) = Lean
Stoichiometric air-fuel ratio = 1
Greater than 1 (1.001 to 1.999) = Rich

entry.ptxt: Ratio of evaporative purge flow to intake air volume:
Approximately 100 kPa: Ignition switch to the ON position
EVAP system pressure monitored by canister pressure sensor
Approximately 100 kPa: Ignition switch to the ON position
EVAP system pressure monitored by canister pressure sensor
Absolute Accelerator Pedal Position (APP) sensor No. 1:
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
2.6 to 4.5 V: Accelerator pedal fully depressed
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
3.4 to 5.0 V: Accelerator pedal fully depressed
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
Whether or not accelerator pedal position sensor detecting idle:
Accelerator fully closed learning value No. 1:
Accelerator fully closed learning value No. 2:
Whether or not fail safe function executed:
Whether or not fail safe function executed:

item.ptxt: 10 to 22%: Throttle fully closed
66 to 98%: Throttle fully open
Calculated value based on VTA1
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)

entry.ptxt: Whether or not throttle position sensor detecting idle:

item.ptxt: 0%: Throttle fully closed
50 to 80%: Throttle fully open
Recognition value for throttle opening angle on ECM
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
42 to 62%: Throttle fully closed
92 to 100%: Throttle fully open
Calculated value based on VTA2
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)

entry.ptxt: Throttle position sensor No. 1 output voltage:

item.ptxt: 0.5 to 1.1 V: Throttle fully closed
3.3 to 4.9 V: Throttle fully open

entry.ptxt: Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
Throttle position sensor No. 2 output voltage:

item.ptxt: 2.1 to 3.1 V: Throttle fully closed
4.6 to 5.0 V: Throttle fully open

entry.ptxt: Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
Throttle position sensor No. 1 output voltage (AD):
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
Whether or not throttle actuator control permitted:
Read value with ignition switch to the ON position (Do not start engine)
Heated oxygen sensor output voltage for sensor 2:
0.1 to 0.9 V: Driving at 44 mph (70 km/h)
Performing INJ VOL or A/F CONTROL function of ACTIVE TEST enables technician to check output voltage of sensor
A/F sensor output voltage for sensor 1:
Performing INJ VOL or A/F CONTROL function of ACTIVE TEST enables technician to check output voltage of sensor
Total fuel trim value for fuel system:
Short-term fuel compensation used to maintain air-fuel ratio at stoichiometric air-fuel ratio
Overall fuel compensation carried out in long-term to compensate for a continual deviation of short-term fuel trim from central value
OL or CL or OL DRIVE or OL FAULT or CL FAULT

item.ptxt: OL (Open Loop): Has not yet satisfied conditions to go closed loop
CL (Closed Loop): Using A/F sensor as feedback for fuel control
OL DRIVE: Open loop due to driving conditions (fuel enrichment)
OL FAULT: Open loop due to detected system fault
CL FAULT: Closed loop but A/F sensor, which used for fuel control malfunctioning

entry.ptxt: OL or CL or OL DRIVE or OL FAULT or CL FAULT
Short-term fuel trim associated with sensor 2:
Short-term fuel trim associated with sensor 1:

item.ptxt: Value less than 1 (0.000 to 0.999) =Lean
Stoichiometric air-fuel ratio=1
Value greater than 1 (1.001 to 1.999) = Rich

entry.ptxt: Engine coolant temperature at engine start:
Intake air temperature at engine start:
Quantity of fuel injection volume for 10 times
ON: When steering wheel first turned after battery terminals connected
Signal status usually ON until battery terminals disconnected

item.ptxt: ON: Throttle fully closed
OFF: Throttle open

entry.ptxt: ON: Headlights or defogger turned ON
Whether or not electric throttle control system power inputted:
ON: Ignition switch to the ON position and system normal
9 to 14 (V): Ignition switch to the ON position and system normal
9 to 14 V: Ignition switch to the ON position
Approximately 100 kPa: Ignition switch to the ON position
Key-off EVAP system leak detection pump status:
Key-off EVAP system vent valve status:
TC and CG (TE1) terminals of DLC3:
Engine rpm during No. 1 cylinder fuel cut:
Output only when FUEL CUT #1 is performed using ACTIVE TEST
Engine rpm during No. 2 cylinder fuel cut:
Output only when FUEL CUT #2 is performed using ACTIVE TEST
Engine rpm during No. 3 cylinder fuel cut:
Output only when FUEL CUT #3 is performed using ACTIVE TEST
Engine rpm during No. 4 cylinder fuel cut:
Output only when FUEL CUT #4 is performed using ACTIVE TEST
Average of engine rpm values during fuel cut of No. 1 to No. 4 cylinders:
Output only when ACTIVE TEST is performed
VVT duty signal value during intrusive operation
Requested duty value for intrusive operation
FC IDL = "ON" when throttle valve fully closed and engine speed over 3,500 rpm
Fuel cut TAU (Fuel cut with a very light load):
Fuel cut being performed with a very light load to prevent incomplete engine combustion
Check mode result for vehicle speed sensor:
Check mode result for misfire monitor:
Check mode result for HO2 sensor:
Check mode result for air-fuel ratio sensor:
Equivalent to running time after MIL ON
Equivalent to time after DTCs erased
Equivalent to drive distance after DTCs erased
Number of warm-up cycles after DTC cleared

item.ptxt: *1: Automatic transaxle only
*2: DATA LIST values are only displayed when performing the following ACTIVE TEST: VVT B1. For other ACTIVE TESTs, the DATA LIST value will be 0.

atten4.ptxt: Performing an ACTIVE TEST enables components including the relays, VSV (Vacuum Switching Valve) and actuators, to be operated without removing any parts. The ACTIVE TEST can be performed with the intelligent tester. Performing the ACTIVE TEST as the first step in troubleshooting is one method to shorten diagnostic time.

item.ptxt: DATA LIST can be displayed during ACTIVE TEST.

step2.ptxt: Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Turn the ignition switch to the ON position.
Select the following menu items: DIAGNOSIS / ENHANCED OBD II / ACTIVE TEST.
Perform the ACTIVE TEST by referring to the table below.

item.ptxt: All injectors tested at same time
Perform test at less than 3,000 rpm
Injection volume can be changed in 0.1% graduations within control range
Perform test at less than 3,000 rpm
A/F CONTROL enables checking and graphing of A/F (Air-Fuel Ratio) sensor and Heated Oxygen (HO2) sensor voltage outputs
To conduct test, select following menu items: ACTIVE TEST / A/F CONTROL / USER DATA / AFS B1S1 and O2S B1S2; then press YES and ENTER followed by F4.

entry.ptxt: Test is possible when engine stopped
Turn on and off TC and TE1 connection

item.ptxt: ON: TC and TE1 connected
OFF: TC and TE1 disconnected

entry.ptxt: Test is possible when following conditions met:

item.ptxt: Engine stopped
Shift position in P
Fully depressing accelerator pedal (APP: 59° or more)

entry.ptxt: This value added to present OCV control duty

item.ptxt: Engine stall or rough idle when VVT actuator operated by 100%.
Test is possible while vehicle stopped and engine idling

entry.ptxt: Test is possible while vehicle stopped and engine idling
Active IMRV (Intake Manifold Runner Valve) motor

item.ptxt: When the duty ratio is -100% IMRV closes
When the duty ratio is 100% IMRV opens
Test is possible while vehicle stopped and engine idling
ON: All cylinder injector fuel cut and ignition stop

entry.ptxt: All cylinder injector fuel cut and ignition stop

atten4.ptxt: *: When cranking the engine, each cylinder measures the engine rpm.

In this ACTIVE TEST, the fuel and ignition of all cylinders is cut, and cranking occurs for approximately 10 seconds. Then, each cylinder measures the engine rpm. If a cylinder's engine rpm is higher than the others, that cylinder's compression pressure is compared to the others, and whether or not the compression pressure is low can be determined.

item.ptxt: Warm up the engine.
Turn the ignition switch off.
Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Turn the ignition switch to the ON position and turn the intelligent tester on.
Select the following menu items: DIAGNOSIS / ENHANCED OBD II / ACTIVE TEST / COMPRESS CHECK.

atten4.ptxt: If the results are not displayed normally, select the display items from the DATA LIST before performing the ACTIVE TEST. Select the following menu items: DIAGNOSIS / ENHANCED OBD II / DATA LIST / USER DATA / ENG SPEED #1 and ENG SPEED #2, ENG SPEED #3, ENG SPEED #4 (Press the YES button to change the ENG SPEED #1 to #4) and then press the ENTER button.

item.ptxt: While the engine is not running, press the RIGHT or LEFT button to change the COMPRESS CHECK to ON.

atten4.ptxt: After performing the above procedure, the ACTIVE TEST's COMPRESS CHECK will start. Fuel injection for all cylinders is prohibited, and each cylinder's engine rpm measurement will enter standby mode.

item.ptxt: Fully open the throttle.
Crank the engine for about 10 seconds.
Monitor the engine speed (ENG SPEED #1 to #4) displayed on the tester.

atten4.ptxt: At first, the tester's display will show each cylinder's engine rpm measurement to be extremely high. After approximately 10 seconds of engine cranking, each cylinder's engine rpm measurement will change to the actual engine rpm.

item.ptxt: After the ACTIVE TEST's COMPRESS CHECK is turned ON, it will automatically turn off after 255 seconds.
When the COMPRESS CHECK test is OFF and the engine is cranked, the engine will start.
If the COMPRESS CHECK test needs to be performed after it is turned ON and performed once, press EXIT to return to the ACTIVE TEST menu screen. Then perform the COMPRESS CHECK test again.
Use a fully-charged battery.

atten4.ptxt: Performing a SYSTEM CHECK enables the system, which consists of multiple actuators, to be operated without removing any parts. In addition, it can show whether or not any DTCs are set, and can detect potential malfunctions in the system. The SYSTEM CHECK can be performed with the intelligent tester.

step2.ptxt: Connect the intelligent tester to the DLC3.
Turn the ignition switch to the ON position.
Select the following menu items: DIAGNOSIS / ENHANCED OBD II / SYSTEM CHECK.
Perform the SYSTEM CHECK by referring to the table below.

entry.ptxt: Long: Indicates fuel consumption for maximum 60 sec.
Short: Indicates fuel consumption for maximum 20 sec.
While driving (vehicle speed 3.1 mph (5 km/h) or more)
Perform 5 steps in order to operate EVAP key-off monitor automatically
Perform 5 steps in order to operate EVAP key-off monitor manually
